Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). ALT is mildly elevated. These findings suggest significant renal dysfunction, likely acute or chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular damage and impaired concentrating ability. The presence of glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate poor glycemic control or possible diabetes. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.
